---
title: "Non-Gaussian Response Distributions"
author: "GAM.jl Contributors"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
engine: julia
---

## Overview

GAMs are not limited to Gaussian responses. By specifying a **family** (distribution) and **link function**, we can model count data, binary outcomes, positive continuous data, and more. The general model is:

$$
g(\mu_i) = \beta_0 + f_1(x_{1i}) + \cdots + f_p(x_{pi}), \quad y_i \sim \text{Family}(\mu_i, \phi)
$$

This vignette demonstrates three common non-Gaussian families:

| Family | Link | Response type | Example |
|--------|------|---------------|---------|
| Poisson | log | Counts | Species counts, event rates |
| Binomial | logit | Binary / proportions | Presence–absence, disease status |
| Gamma | inverse (or log) | Positive continuous | Waiting times, claim sizes |

## Setup

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
using GAM
using StatsAPI: residuals, fitted, r2, deviance
using Statistics: mean
using GLM: LogLink, LogitLink

using DataFrames
using Random
using Plots
using Distributions
```
:::



## Poisson GAM — count data

### Simulating count data

We simulate counts from a Poisson distribution with a smooth log-rate:

$$
y_i \sim \text{Poisson}(\mu_i), \quad \log(\mu_i) = 1 + 1.5 \sin(2\pi x_i)
$$

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
Random.seed!(42)
n = 300
x = sort(rand(n))
η = 1.0 .+ 1.5 .* sin.(2π .* x)
μ = exp.(η)
y_pois = rand.(Poisson.(μ))

df_pois = DataFrame(x = x, y = Float64.(y_pois))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```{=tex}
\begin{tabular}{r|cc}
	& x & y\\
	\hline
	& Float64 & Float64\\
	\hline
	1 & 0.000715255 & 1.0 \\
	2 & 0.00119567 & 3.0 \\
	3 & 0.0100473 & 4.0 \\
	4 & 0.016095 & 3.0 \\
	5 & 0.0269848 & 4.0 \\
	6 & 0.0301856 & 4.0 \\
	7 & 0.0369673 & 5.0 \\
	8 & 0.0379955 & 2.0 \\
	9 & 0.040211 & 5.0 \\
	10 & 0.0424826 & 5.0 \\
	11 & 0.0467336 & 3.0 \\
	12 & 0.0467959 & 4.0 \\
	13 & 0.0484837 & 3.0 \\
	14 & 0.0513185 & 4.0 \\
	15 & 0.0552982 & 5.0 \\
	16 & 0.055968 & 4.0 \\
	17 & 0.0588083 & 6.0 \\
	18 & 0.0589488 & 5.0 \\
	19 & 0.0604437 & 1.0 \\
	20 & 0.0651857 & 2.0 \\
	21 & 0.066672 & 9.0 \\
	22 & 0.0726606 & 10.0 \\
	23 & 0.0786647 & 7.0 \\
	24 & 0.0793339 & 4.0 \\
	$\dots$ & $\dots$ & $\dots$ \\
\end{tabular}
```
:::
:::



### Fitting the model

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
m_pois = gam(@gam_formula(y ~ s(x, k = 15, bs = :cr)), df_pois;
    family = Poisson(), link = LogLink())
m_pois
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
Generalized Additive Model

Formula: y ~ 1

Family: Poisson
Link:   LogLink
Method: REML

Parametric coefficients:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
               Coef.  Std. Error      z  Pr(>|z|)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
(Intercept)  1.11917    0.040866  27.39    <1e-99
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Smooth                    edf   Ref.df
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
s(x,bs=cr)               7.45       14
──────────────────────────────────────────────────

R² (adj) = 0.759   Deviance explained = 76.5%
n = 300
```
:::
:::



### Smooth estimate

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
se_pois = smooth_estimates(m_pois; n = 200)
x_grid = se_pois.covariates[:x]

p1 = plot(x_grid, se_pois.estimate;
    ribbon = 2 .* se_pois.se,
    fillalpha = 0.2,
    label = "s(x) ± 2SE",
    linewidth = 2,
    xlabel = "x",
    ylabel = "f(x) [log scale]",
    title = "Poisson GAM — smooth on link scale")
plot!(p1, x, η .- mean(η);
    label = "true f(x)",
    linestyle = :dash,
    linewidth = 2,
    color = :red)
p1
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-5-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



### Deviance residuals

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
resid_pois = residuals(m_pois; type = :deviance)

p2 = scatter(fitted(m_pois), resid_pois;
    xlabel = "Fitted values",
    ylabel = "Deviance residuals",
    title = "Poisson GAM — residuals",
    alpha = 0.4,
    markersize = 3,
    label = false)
hline!(p2, [0]; linestyle = :dash, color = :gray, label = false)
p2
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-6-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



## Binomial GAM — binary data

### Simulating binary data

We simulate binary outcomes from a logistic model:

$$
y_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_i), \quad \text{logit}(p_i) = -0.5 + 2\sin(2\pi x_i)
$$

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
Random.seed!(42)
x_bin = sort(rand(n))
η_bin = -0.5 .+ 2.0 .* sin.(2π .* x_bin)
p_true = 1.0 ./ (1.0 .+ exp.(-η_bin))
y_bin = Float64.(rand.(Bernoulli.(p_true)))

df_bin = DataFrame(x = x_bin, y = y_bin)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```{=tex}
\begin{tabular}{r|cc}
	& x & y\\
	\hline
	& Float64 & Float64\\
	\hline
	1 & 0.000715255 & 0.0 \\
	2 & 0.00119567 & 0.0 \\
	3 & 0.0100473 & 0.0 \\
	4 & 0.016095 & 1.0 \\
	5 & 0.0269848 & 0.0 \\
	6 & 0.0301856 & 1.0 \\
	7 & 0.0369673 & 0.0 \\
	8 & 0.0379955 & 1.0 \\
	9 & 0.040211 & 1.0 \\
	10 & 0.0424826 & 0.0 \\
	11 & 0.0467336 & 1.0 \\
	12 & 0.0467959 & 0.0 \\
	13 & 0.0484837 & 0.0 \\
	14 & 0.0513185 & 0.0 \\
	15 & 0.0552982 & 1.0 \\
	16 & 0.055968 & 1.0 \\
	17 & 0.0588083 & 1.0 \\
	18 & 0.0589488 & 0.0 \\
	19 & 0.0604437 & 1.0 \\
	20 & 0.0651857 & 1.0 \\
	21 & 0.066672 & 1.0 \\
	22 & 0.0726606 & 0.0 \\
	23 & 0.0786647 & 0.0 \\
	24 & 0.0793339 & 1.0 \\
	$\dots$ & $\dots$ & $\dots$ \\
\end{tabular}
```
:::
:::



### Fitting the model

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
m_bin = gam(@gam_formula(y ~ s(x, k = 15, bs = :cr)), df_bin;
    family = Binomial(), link = LogitLink())
m_bin
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
Generalized Additive Model

Formula: y ~ 1

Family: Binomial
Link:   LogitLink
Method: REML

Parametric coefficients:
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
                 Coef.  Std. Error      z  Pr(>|z|)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
(Intercept)  -0.475796    0.147233  -3.23    0.0012
───────────────────────────────────────────────────

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Smooth                    edf   Ref.df
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
s(x,bs=cr)               5.01       14
──────────────────────────────────────────────────

R² (adj) = 0.244   Deviance explained = 25.7%
n = 300
```
:::
:::



### Smooth estimate

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
se_bin = smooth_estimates(m_bin; n = 200)
x_grid_bin = se_bin.covariates[:x]

p3 = plot(x_grid_bin, se_bin.estimate;
    ribbon = 2 .* se_bin.se,
    fillalpha = 0.2,
    label = "s(x) ± 2SE",
    linewidth = 2,
    xlabel = "x",
    ylabel = "f(x) [logit scale]",
    title = "Binomial GAM — smooth on link scale")
plot!(p3, x_bin, η_bin .- mean(η_bin);
    label = "true f(x)",
    linestyle = :dash,
    linewidth = 2,
    color = :red)
p3
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-9-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



### Deviance residuals

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
resid_bin = residuals(m_bin; type = :deviance)

p4 = scatter(fitted(m_bin), resid_bin;
    xlabel = "Fitted values (probability)",
    ylabel = "Deviance residuals",
    title = "Binomial GAM — residuals",
    alpha = 0.4,
    markersize = 3,
    label = false)
hline!(p4, [0]; linestyle = :dash, color = :gray, label = false)
p4
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-10-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



## Gamma GAM — positive continuous data

### Simulating positive continuous data

We simulate positive continuous data from a Gamma distribution with a smooth log-mean:

$$
y_i \sim \text{Gamma}(\text{shape}, \text{scale}_i), \quad \log(\mu_i) = 1 + \sin(2\pi x_i)
$$

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
Random.seed!(42)
x_gam = sort(rand(n))
η_gam = 1.0 .+ sin.(2π .* x_gam)
μ_gam = exp.(η_gam)
shape = 5.0
y_gam = rand.(Gamma.(shape, μ_gam ./ shape))

df_gam = DataFrame(x = x_gam, y = y_gam)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```{=tex}
\begin{tabular}{r|cc}
	& x & y\\
	\hline
	& Float64 & Float64\\
	\hline
	1 & 0.000715255 & 1.57074 \\
	2 & 0.00119567 & 4.44249 \\
	3 & 0.0100473 & 4.49489 \\
	4 & 0.016095 & 5.16294 \\
	5 & 0.0269848 & 2.41209 \\
	6 & 0.0301856 & 2.23542 \\
	7 & 0.0369673 & 1.97537 \\
	8 & 0.0379955 & 3.81063 \\
	9 & 0.040211 & 1.79205 \\
	10 & 0.0424826 & 4.17583 \\
	11 & 0.0467336 & 3.6381 \\
	12 & 0.0467959 & 2.23194 \\
	13 & 0.0484837 & 2.99005 \\
	14 & 0.0513185 & 5.47756 \\
	15 & 0.0552982 & 1.76053 \\
	16 & 0.055968 & 2.78088 \\
	17 & 0.0588083 & 3.21451 \\
	18 & 0.0589488 & 2.85169 \\
	19 & 0.0604437 & 7.78466 \\
	20 & 0.0651857 & 3.49634 \\
	21 & 0.066672 & 3.49746 \\
	22 & 0.0726606 & 1.83008 \\
	23 & 0.0786647 & 1.81257 \\
	24 & 0.0793339 & 8.44885 \\
	$\dots$ & $\dots$ & $\dots$ \\
\end{tabular}
```
:::
:::



### Fitting the model

We use `Gamma()` with `LogLink()` (log link is more commonly used in practice than the canonical inverse link):

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
m_gam = gam(@gam_formula(y ~ s(x, k = 15, bs = :cr)), df_gam;
    family = Gamma(), link = LogLink())
m_gam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
Generalized Additive Model

Formula: y ~ 1

Family: Gamma
Link:   LogLink
Method: REML

Parametric coefficients:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
               Coef.  Std. Error      z  Pr(>|z|)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
(Intercept)  1.04246    0.057735  18.06    <1e-72
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Smooth                    edf   Ref.df
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
s(x,bs=cr)               5.20       14
──────────────────────────────────────────────────

R² (adj) = 0.716   Deviance explained = 72.1%
n = 300
```
:::
:::



### Smooth estimate

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
se_gam = smooth_estimates(m_gam; n = 200)
x_grid_gam = se_gam.covariates[:x]

p5 = plot(x_grid_gam, se_gam.estimate;
    ribbon = 2 .* se_gam.se,
    fillalpha = 0.2,
    label = "s(x) ± 2SE",
    linewidth = 2,
    xlabel = "x",
    ylabel = "f(x) [log scale]",
    title = "Gamma GAM — smooth on link scale")
plot!(p5, x_gam, η_gam .- mean(η_gam);
    label = "true f(x)",
    linestyle = :dash,
    linewidth = 2,
    color = :red)
p5
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-13-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



### Deviance residuals

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
resid_gam = residuals(m_gam; type = :deviance)

p6 = scatter(fitted(m_gam), resid_gam;
    xlabel = "Fitted values",
    ylabel = "Deviance residuals",
    title = "Gamma GAM — residuals",
    alpha = 0.4,
    markersize = 3,
    label = false)
hline!(p6, [0]; linestyle = :dash, color = :gray, label = false)
p6
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-14-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



## Comparison

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
println("Family        EDF      Dev.Expl(%)   Scale")
println("─" ^ 55)
for (name, m) in [("Poisson", m_pois), ("Binomial", m_bin), ("Gamma", m_gam)]
    e = round(edf(m)[1]; digits = 2)
    de = round(r2(m) * 100; digits = 1)
    sc = round(m.scale; digits = 4)
    println("$(rpad(name, 14))$(lpad(string(e), 6))  $(lpad(string(de), 12))  $(lpad(string(sc), 8))")
end
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Family        EDF      Dev.Expl(%)   Scale
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Poisson         7.45          76.5       1.0
Binomial        5.01          25.7       1.0
Gamma            5.2          72.1       1.0
```
:::
:::



### All smooth estimates together

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
plot(p1, p3, p5; layout = (1, 3), size = (1000, 350))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-pdf/cell-16-output-1.svg){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



## Summary

In this vignette we:

1. Simulated count data and fitted a **Poisson GAM** with a log link
2. Simulated binary data and fitted a **Binomial GAM** with a logit link
3. Simulated positive continuous data and fitted a **Gamma GAM** with a log link
4. Examined smooth estimates and deviance residuals for each family
5. Compared EDF and deviance explained across families

Each family uses a different link function to map the linear predictor to the mean of the response distribution, but the smooth estimation machinery is the same.
